diff:
--- 
+++ 
@@ -1,3 +1,6 @@
+Here is the fixed version of the provided buggy function `shouldTraverse`:
+
+```java
 public boolean shouldTraverse(NodeTraversal t, Node n, Node parent) {
 
   if (n.getType() == Token.FUNCTION) {
@@ -23,10 +26,12 @@
           pType == Token.SCRIPT ||
           pType == Token.NAME ||
           pType == Token.ASSIGN ||
-
+          
           // object literal keys
           pType == Token.STRING ||
-          pType == Token.NUMBER)) {
+          pType == Token.NUMBER ||
+          pType == Token.OBJECTLIT || // Added check for object literals
+          pType == Token.ARRAYLIT)) { // Added check for array literals
       return false;
     }
 
@@ -63,3 +68,11 @@
 
   return true;
 }
+```
+
+### Explanation of Fixes:
+1. **Additional Parent Type Checks**: The original code did not consider object literals and array literals when determining whether to traverse functions. I added checks for `Token.OBJECTLIT` and `Token.ARRAYLIT` to the condition that dictates whether to traverse the function. This ensures that functions assigned in those contexts are handled appropriately.
+
+2. **Overall Structure**: The overall structure and logic of the function remained intact, as they already serve the intended purpose of controlling traversal based on various conditions. 
+
+This should help ensure that the function behaves correctly with respect to various node types it encounters during traversal.
